mainEntity.name: How much does a water heater replacement cost in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard 50-gallon gas tank water heater costs $1,200 to $2,500 installed. Electric tanks run $1,000 to $2,200. Tankless gas units cost $3,000 to $5,500, and heat pump water heaters cost $2,500 to $4,500 (the 25C federal tax credit expired Dec 31 2025; state utility rebates may still apply).

mainEntity.name: How much does a whole-house repipe cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A PEX repipe for a typical 2,000-sq-ft home runs $4,500 to $10,000. Copper repipes cost $8,000 to $16,000 for the same size home. The main cost drivers are home size, number of fixtures, access (slab vs. basement), and pipe material.

mainEntity.name: Is PEX piping as good as copper?

acceptedAnswer.text: For most residential applications, yes. PEX is flexible, freeze-resistant, corrosion-proof, and significantly less expensive than copper. It has been used in Europe since the 1970s and in the US since the 1990s with an excellent track record.

mainEntity.name: How long does a sewer line replacement take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Traditional open-trench sewer line replacement takes 2 to 4 days. Trenchless methods (pipe bursting or pipe lining) can often be completed in 1 to 2 days with minimal yard disruption.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a permit for plumbing work?

acceptedAnswer.text: In most jurisdictions, yes, for anything beyond a simple fixture swap. Water heater replacements, repipes, sewer work, and new drain lines almost always require a plumbing permit and inspection.

mainEntity.name: Should I switch from a tank to a tankless water heater?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on your household. Tankless heaters provide unlimited hot water and last 20+ years, but they cost more upfront and may require gas line or electrical upgrades. For households with high hot water demand or limited space, tankless is often worth the investment.
